paper_title: Evaluation of Climate Models Against Proxy Data and Idealized Climate Model Perturbation Experiments

methods.method_name: Evaluation of Climate Models Against Proxy Data
methods.description: This method evaluates climate model simulations against reconstructed proxy data to estimate global-scale temperatures from high-latitude proxy data. It involves comparing model simulations with four different proxy datasets to assess the correlation and discrepancies between them.

steps.description: Select the same grids covered by the proxy data in the climate models.
steps.searchable_summary: select climate model grids matching proxy data locations

steps.description: Calculate spatial correlation coefficients between the proxy data and the models.
Average annual mean temperature anomalies over the selected grids.
steps.searchable_summary: average temperature anomalies over selected grids

steps.description: Compare proxy-based temperature anomaly with climate models and assess uncertainty range using Monte Carlo simulation.
steps.searchable_summary: compare proxy-based anomalies with models and assess uncertainty

methods.description: This method involves conducting idealized climate model simulations to test the impact of Arctic sea ice loss and tropical SST cooling on extratropical temperature during the mid-Holocene.

steps.description: Perform mid-Holocene climate simulation with increased sea ice albedo (316-year duration).
steps.searchable_summary: run mid-Holocene simulation with increased sea ice albedo

steps.description: Analyze the contributions of Arctic sea ice loss and insolation forcing using the last 250 years of simulation data.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze contributions of sea ice loss and insolation forcing

steps.description: Perform mid-Holocene climate simulation with tropical SSTs restored to preindustrial levels (180-year duration).
steps.searchable_summary: run mid-Holocene simulation with restored tropical SSTs

steps.description: Analyze the contribution of tropical SST cooling using the last 150 years of simulation data.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze contribution of tropical SST cooling
